title: Stream Record
description: An envelope describing a single record on a stream — the unit of data that producers append and consumers read. Generalizes Kafka ProducerRecord/ConsumerRecord, Pulsar Message, Kinesis Record, and Pub/Sub PubsubMessage.

stream.description: Identifier of the stream / topic this record belongs to.

partition.description: Partition / shard identifier the record was written to.

offset.description: Position of the record within the partition (integer offset, Pulsar MessageId, Kinesis sequence number, Pub/Sub message id).

key.description: Optional record key used for partition assignment and log-compaction identity. Null indicates an unkeyed record.

value.description: Record payload. May be a JSON object, a base64-encoded binary blob, or a string, depending on encoding.

value_encoding.description: How the value field is encoded.

headers.description: User-supplied metadata key/value pairs attached to the record.

event_time.description: When the event actually occurred, per the producer.

ingestion_time.description: When the streaming platform appended the record.

schema_id.description: Schema Registry id used to deserialize the value.

transaction_id.description: Producer transaction id for transactional writes (exactly-once).

trace_context.description: OpenTelemetry / W3C Trace Context propagated via headers.
